name: Justice Finance Co
description_short: Justice Finance is a privately owned personal loan lender in the Dallas-Fort Worth area offering signature loans up to $1,800 with flexible payment terms and no checking account requirement.
description_long:
Justice Finance is a privately owned financing firm operating across the Dallas-Fort Worth (DFW) metroplex with 18 physical locations. Founded on principles of responsibility and integrity, the company positions itself as a mainstream personal loan provider distinct from payday lenders and online-only operators.

Justice Finance offers signature loans up to $1,800 with no collateral required and no checking account prerequisite. They provide flexible payment options tailored to borrower budgets and advertise competitive interest rates. The application process includes three steps: online or in-person application, validation by loan experts, and approval (subject to credit requirements). Customers can apply online, by phone, or in-person at any of their 18 DFW locations.

The company differentiates itself through an emphasis on customer service and willingness to work with borrowers who have imperfect credit. Testimonials highlight responsive staff, quick approval processes, and personalized assistance. They explicitly state they are "not a payday loan company or online lender," suggesting a focus on traditional installment lending rather than high-fee short-term products.

As a regional, brick-and-mortar lender, Justice Finance serves a niche market but lacks the transparency typical of modern lenders. The website provides no specific APR ranges, loan terms, repayment schedules, or eligibility requirements—only asterisked disclaimers that these "vary." No independent verification of claims about rates or service quality is available on the site itself. Borrowers must visit a location or call for concrete pricing.
address: 5432 E Grand Ave B, Dallas, TX 75223

company_info.headquarters: 5432 E Grand Ave B, Dallas, TX 75223

pros: No checking account required to qualify, lowering barriers for unbanked borrowers
Signature loans with no collateral or security requirements
18 physical locations throughout Dallas-Fort Worth for in-person support
Explicitly not a payday lender—positioned as traditional installment lender with better terms
Flexible payment options designed to fit individual budgets
Willing to work with borrowers who have poor or bad credit
Fast approval and funding process with same-location fund pickup option
cons: Loan amount capped at $1,800, limiting use for larger expenses or consolidation
Website provides no specific APR ranges, interest rates, or loan terms—all listed as variable with no baseline disclosed
Regional availability limited to Dallas-Fort Worth metroplex only; no online or national lending option
No transparent eligibility criteria or qualification thresholds published
Limited digital presence—requires in-person visit or phone call to get actual pricing and loan details
best_for: Unbanked or underbanked borrowers in DFW without traditional checking accounts
Borrowers with bad or poor credit seeking alternatives to payday loans
Consumers needing small loans ($500–$1,800) with flexible repayment and local support
Applicants preferring in-person interaction and relationship-based lending over fully online platforms
diagnosis: Justice Finance is best for credit-challenged borrowers in the Dallas-Fort Worth area seeking small personal loans ($1K–$1.8K) with flexible terms and willing staff. The main caveat is complete lack of pricing transparency—actual APR, interest rates, and loan terms are not disclosed online, requiring in-person or phone contact to compare against alternatives.
services: Online loan applications with fast validation
In-person branch applications at 18 DFW locations
Flexible payment plans customized to borrower budget
No collateral or security requirement on loans
Loan approval subject to credit review
Same-day or next-business-day fund availability after approval
Customer service and loan counseling at branch locations
Social media support and community engagement
